item.title: AI Is Being Built to Replace You—Not Help You
item.description: Nobel Prize–winning economist Daron Acemoglu warns that multiple factors are driving a labor-replacement model, with potentially dire consequences for society.

item.title: Meeting Surging Demand for AI Memory Chips Has a Climate Cost
item.description: The rush to boost production of memory chips to meet fast accelerating demand from artificial intelligence will add to the semiconductor sector’s climate footprint and risks lifting costs of managing emissions.

item.title: Wells Fargo Is Said to Hire UBS’s Keller to Lead M&A Structuring
item.description: Wells Fargo & Co. has hired Derek Keller from UBS Group AG as a managing director and head of M&A structuring, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Japan Stock Traders’ Guide to Navigating Takaichi-Trump Summit
item.description: Japan’s equity investors will closely watch a meeting between President Donald Trump and Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi in Washington on Thursday for possible agreements on economic and military cooperation.

item.title: Gold Steadies After Six-Day Decline as Fed Warns on Inflation
item.description: Gold steadied, after tumbling nearly 4% in the previous session, with the Federal Reserve warning of inflation risks tied to the Middle East war and surging energy prices.

item.title: Asian Stocks to Fall as Iran War Saps Sentiment: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian equities faced early declines Thursday as signs of escalating tensions in the Middle East drove oil higher and weighed on US stocks and bonds.

item.title: Powell Says War Too Soon to Judge as Inflation Keeps Fed on Hold
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell made it clear the US central bank won’t cut interest rates again until inflation resumes cooling. And that’s before it even starts considering the possible impact of the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Oil Advances After Iran, Israel Strike Major Energy Assets
item.description: Oil rose after Iran and Israel traded strikes on key energy facilities in the Middle East, boosting turmoil in markets as the conflict stretched toward three weeks.

item.title: Quebec Projects Smaller Deficit, Aims to Balance Budget by 2030
item.description: The Canadian province of Quebec says it will trim its budget deficit in the years ahead as it brings in more tax revenue and restrains new spending, despite trade tensions with the US.

item.title: Taiwan Central Bank Seen Holding Key Rate as Iran War Unfolds
item.description: Taiwan will likely leave its key policy rate unchanged for the eighth straight quarter — the longest run since 2019 — while it monitors the economic impact of the Iran War.

item.title: Presidential Candidate Says Colombia Must Bet on Oil and Mining
item.description: Colombian presidential candidate Paloma Valencia said that investments in oil, natural gas and mining can lift its people out of poverty.

item.title: New Zealand Economy Slows More Than Expected Before Iran War Hit
item.description: New Zealand’s economic growth slowed more than expected in the final three months of 2025, leaving it in a weaker position to absorb fallout from the spiraling conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Brazil Cuts Interest Rate to 14.75% in Careful Move as War Blurs Outlook
item.description: Brazil’s central bank cut its key interest rate by a quarter-point in the first reduction since 2024, as the economy cools while war in the Middle East raises inflation concerns.

item.title: Silver Lake-Backed Relativity Said to Tap Banks for US IPO
item.description: Relativity, a legal data firm backed by Silver Lake, has picked banks to help arrange for an initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Five Below Tops Full-Year Outlook Aided by Store Openings
item.description: Shares of Five Below Inc. rose 7% in post-market trade after it issued a better-than-expected fiscal 2026 outlook and predicted a sales bump fueled by new store openings.

item.title: Cliffwater Private Credit Fund’s Outlook Cut to Negative by S&P
item.description: S&P Global Ratings lowered its outlook on Cliffwater LLC’s flagship private credit fund to negative from stable, citing elevated redemption requests that risk putting pressure on its liquidity.

item.title: Oil Driller Maha Exercises Venezuela Rights After US Clearance
item.description: Maha Capital AB, a Swedish energy company managed by Brazilian executives, has exercised its rights to acquire a 24% stake in a Venezuelan oil field after the US provided sanctions relief.

item.title: The Dot Plot, Explained: How the Fed Forecasts Interest Rates
item.description: It’s almost certainly the most closely scrutinized scatter chart in financial markets. Every three months since January 2012, the Federal Reserve has sent analysts scurrying by updating its “dot plot,” which has become the de facto interest rate forecast of the US central bank — whether the Fed wants it to be or not. It’s also an important if cryptic source of clues to any dissent among Fed policymakers.

item.title: Fannie and Freddie Shares Tank as Doubt Swirls About Trump Plans
item.description: Shares of Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac, down about 70% in the last six months, hit their lowest level in more than a year as investors cast doubt on the Trump administration’s efforts to sell more stock in the mortgage-finance giants to the public.

item.title: NTT Global Data Centers Plans to Double Capacity in AI Boom
item.description: NTT Global Data Centers, the world’s third-largest data center provider outside of China, is working to double its capacity to 4 gigawatts to meet the rising global demand for the critical digital infrastructure amid an artificial intelligence boom.

item.title: Foreign Holdings of US Treasuries Rose in January, Led by Japan
item.description: Overseas holdings of US Treasuries rose in January, a month that saw a spike in concern over European demand for American assets in light of President Donald Trump’s threats over Greenland.

item.title: The 2-Year Yield Is the Chart of the Day
item.description: All you need to know about the FOMC.

item.title: Argentina Posts Highest Fourth-Quarter Unemployment Since Pandemic
item.description: Argentina’s unemployment rate rose to 7.5% at the end of last year, the highest fourth-quarter reading since the Covid-19 pandemic, as job losses worsened before Javier Milei’s administration passed a historic labor reform last month.

item.title: As Global Oil Prices Skyrocket, US Crude Is Lagging Behind
item.description: Benchmark oil prices are soaring as war in the Middle East roils trading flows, but the rally is uneven across the globe, sparking some of the largest gaps between US crude and the rest of the world in years.

item.title: Micron Gives Upbeat Forecast After Demand Soars for Memory Chips
item.description: Micron Technology Inc., the largest US maker of computer memory chips, offered a positive forecast for the current quarter after surging prices for the components bolstered its prospects.

item.title: Criminal Charges Amp Up Growing State Resistance to Kalshi
item.description: The rapid expansion of prediction markets is facing increasingly aggressive pushback from state officials pursuing a sprawling patchwork of lawsuits and cease-and-desist orders.

item.title: Powell Says No Plans to Leave Fed Until Investigation Over
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said he plans to stay at the central bank until after the Justice Department’s investigation is complete.

item.title: Venezuela Oil Exports to US Double on Easing Trade Restrictions
item.description: US fuel makers are boosting purchases of crude from Venezuela to the highest in more than a year, making good on the Trump administration’s goal of taking more crude from the country as the Iran war reshuffles global oil flows.

item.title: JPMorgan’s Michele Says Fed Signal Is ‘Don’t Worry About It’
item.description: JPMorgan Investment Management’s Bob Michele said the Federal Reserve sent a “don’t worry about it” signal to markets spooked by surging oil prices and heightened geopolitical risk because of the war in Iran.

item.title: Iran War May Delay Deal Timelines, Not Derail M&A, McMaster Says
item.description: War with Iran may slow the pace of dealmaking but it’s unlikely to dent overall M&A activity, according to Lazard Inc.’s global head of mergers and acquisitions.
